results.title: Arda Cankat Bati
highlights:
Senior ML Engineer | Built & Led End-to-End ML Systems | Spark, Python, AWS | Deep Learning, Forecasting | LLMs & GenAI
...
Senior ML Engineer at [Norfolk Southern](https://www.linkedin.com/company/norfolk-southern)
...
Senior Machine Learning Engineer with 4+ years of experience building high-impact, end-to-end ML systems that scale—from raw data to production deployment. My work spans forecasting, anomaly detection, and infrastructure optimization using Spark, Python, and AWS in production environments. At Norfolk Southern, I’ve been part of and led initiatives that process billions of sensor records, forecast critical rail defects, and detect anomalies. These systems support $M+ operational decisions and have helped significantly reduce risk across one of the largest freight networks in the U.S. I’ve built and maintained distributed ML pipelines using Databricks, AWS, and Spark—while also working closely with stakeholders to align technical delivery with business outcomes. I thrive at the intersection of modeling, data, and infrastructure—and I'm currently building projects with LLMs and GenAI to expand my skill set into new areas of AI innovation. While I’m especially excited about the future of Generative AI, I remain passionate about any ML role that blends technical depth, product impact, and system-level thinking. Core skills: Machine Learning · Deep Learning · Time Series Forecasting · Anomaly Detection · MLOps · Databricks · Spark · Python · AWS · Model Deployment · CI/CD · Data Engineering · Cross-Functional Collaboration · LLMs & GenAI

Being recognized by peers I respect and learn from every day is a powerful reminder of what I’m most proud of: my team. 

Over the years, I’ve had the privilege of building a group of exceptional talent across data, machine learning, computer vision & deep learning, and Gen AI. What sets them apart isn’t just capability: it’s how they operate.
...
It’s incredible to see
...
far we’ve come together… from no AI to industry-leading 400+ AI solutions across train planning, digital inspections, and predictive maintenance, to name a few. From a multitude of fragmented data platforms to a minimal, best-in-class ecosystem with mature governance.
...
Josh Raglin and I will be joining a sustainability panel next month where we’ll be highlighting Norfolk Southern and Josh’s incredible work toward building a better planet, along with how AI has played a measurable role in advancing that mission.
...
A little story here: during our intro call with the host, Josh introduced me as the leader who’s been implementing NS’s AI solutions for the past 12 years. As you might expect, their immediate reaction was: 12 years ago? You were way ahead of the curve on AI before it was even cool.
...
That’s correct…. our AI journey started long before the recent rush to implement AI solutions everywhere. It began with our Engineering department. As many of you know, NS Engineering department led by Ed Boyle , is one of the most well‑run organizations in the industry with the lowest cost‑per‑mile. Beyond their grit,  they are one of the most, if not the most innovative departments at Norfolk Southern.. They were the very first group to reach out to me about what was then a long‑standing Rail Wear Prediction challenge. Fast forward to today; it’s been almost a decade since we started predicting rail wear across the entire network (19,500 miles) up to five years in advance with a very high degree of accuracy. Our Engineering team uses that predictive insight, along with their deep domain expertise and established processes to fundamentally transform rail maintenance; replacing the right rail, at the right time, in the right location. Since that initial success, we’ve continued to partner with Engineering year after year to tackle new high‑value opportunities that enhance safety, service, and productivity.
...
Fast forward to two weeks ago, I had the opportunity to present our latest stealth Gen AI platform, NSIQ, to all of our leaders at the General Management Meeting. The response in the room was overwhelming… It really got the gears turning; people started thinking about the new possibilities for fundamentally transforming how they do business, and building the next frontier of our digital railroad at NS.

results.title: Bradley Duckworth
highlights:
Senior Director, Data Products at [Norfolk Southern](https://www.linkedin.com/company/norfolk-southern)
...
I build enterprise data organizations and AI-enabled decision capabilities in complex, global operating environments. My work sits at the intersection of data strategy, platform modernization, governance, and operational execution, where value is created by translating data and AI into decisions that actually change how a business runs. I currently lead enterprise data products for operations at Norfolk Southern, where I built the function from the ground up: 23-person team spanning data engineering, analytics, and product ownership; migration to Databricks; enterprise governance on Collibra and Unity Catalog; executive reporting cadences (AOP/QBR) used by the C-suite; and a decision science capability (PRISM) that integrates ML forecasting into operational planning, delivering $26M in annual savings. Previously at AWS, I founded a global analytics and inventory management function across 700+ datacenter facilities on three continents, delivering $96M in annual cost reduction. At Amazon, I led product strategy for enterprise e-commerce capabilities serving 30,000+ federal users and supporting a $450M contract. Before that, I spent six years at Maersk in operations and commercial leadership roles across four continents. I am completing the Chief AI Officer program at the University of Chicago Booth School of Business in October 2026. A consistent theme in my career is adapting to new operating contexts and delivering at pace. I have moved from global logistics operations to e-commerce product management to cloud infrastructure analytics to deep-tech entrepreneurship to enterprise data leadership. Each transition required learning a new domain quickly, earning credibility with experts who had been in the space for years, and building or transforming the function I was hired to lead. That adaptability is now directed at data and AI at enterprise scale. Separately, I founded Lunano, a deep-tech startup commercializing nanotechnology licensed from Sandia National Labs for consumer product applications. Our compound was successfully tested by Reckitt-Benckiser for integration into Lysol, where it performed as designed. Three patents granted or pending. The company was dissolved in 2024 when the Fortune 500 partner elected not to advance to full commercial licensing. The experience sharpened how I think about product-market fit, incumbent market dynamics, and executive decision-making in high-uncertainty environments.
...
I lead enterprise data products, analytics, and performance management for operations at Norfolk Southern, where I built the data function from the ground up. Responsibilities span data strategy, platform architecture, governance, executive reporting, and team development. Key initiatives I have led include: migration from legacy on-prem systems to Databricks, including operating model design and team structure for the cloud environment; deployment of enterprise data governance on Collibra and Unity Catalog with KPI certification, Critical Data Element identification, and lineage; consolidation of legacy BI tools into governed self-service on Power BI with Copilot for AI-assisted analytics; and direction of a decision science capability integrating ML forecasting into operational planning systems with measurable business impact. I own AOP and QBR cadences used by senior leadership to manage operational performance and chair the Change Advisory Board. I also serve on the NS-Union Pacific integration planning team, leading cross-platform data exchange design between Databricks and Snowflake environments.

Every analyst has a favorite tool.

I’ve built workflows in Alteryx, written thousands of lines of SQL, and developed Python programs from scratch — including a native desktop application used for emergency research. I don’t have a favorite. I have a decision framework.
...
The data lives in a database
...
should stay there. I need precision and
...
I once used SQL to track movement across the network in real time. I could identify the last departed location, calculate status in route , and specifically flag equipment that were between 2 and 4 hours from their destination. No other tool needed. Just precise
...
applied directly to live data.
...
I reach for Alteryx when:
...
I’m blending multiple data sources and speed of development matters. The workflow needs to be documented visually, handed off to someone non-technical, or deployed to a server to run on a schedule — automatically sending reports, emails, and updating tables without anyone touching it.
...
I reach for Python when:
...
No other tool can handle what I need. Custom logic, API calls, web scraping, aggregating data across odd sources like emails, or building something that needs to scale and be reused. Python is where I go when the problem doesn’t fit a box.
...
’t using the wrong tool. It’s using the
...
for every problem because it’s the one you know best.

results.title: Chris M. Christian
highlights:
Lead DevSecOps Architect at Norfolk Southern Corp
...
Lead DevSecOps Architect at Norfolk Southern Corp at [Norfolk Southern Corporation](https://www.linkedin.com/company/norfolk-southern)
...
My experience starts w/ apps/web design in early 90. I quickly went into infrastructure & loved working w/ the HW. I was writing custom policy templates for NT 3.51/4.0, trying to secure/lock-down standard corporate desktops in the mid 90s. I also implemented centralized servers to store drivers/SW to ease deployment; scripted & simplified imaging the pc's (common place now, cutting edge then). I spent time w/ Crosscom routers & Cabletron switches until Cisco. I architected/deployed our early Citrix farms (Winframe now Metaframe). Moving to Web Services on Sun Solaris I worked w/ WebLogic Clusters. I managed all our IBM AIX & Sun Solaris HW in our data centers (spec'ing, standards, logistics, racking, inventorying, vendor relations). We basically have every flavor of RS-6000,Regatta,P-Series,SP2, etc. I became Lead SAN Architect & worked w/ McData, Hitachi, EMC, IBM. Fabric experience is Intrepid 6140,6064,i10k, Sphereon 3232,4700, Eclipse 2640 & mgt on EFCM w/ Perf/Event/SANav. Storage experience is Lightning 9980,9970, TagmaStor NSC-55, old Symmetrix, new DMX-3, Clariion CX-500,CX-700,CX3-80,Centera, Tape Library 3584 w/LTO-3/2 drives. Storage Mgt w/ HiCommand Device/Tuning Manager, HiTrack,Storage Nav, Replication over Ciena DWDM using True Copy,MirrorView & SRDF w/ McData Eclipse & Sphereon. I ensured all our environments replicated w/ scripts (CCI/NaviCLI). I was a key resource in the moving/buildout of our new prod data center, including our new backbone fabric design & state of the art cable mgt/dist. I spoke on a Gartner Group panel called "Building the high performance data center of the future". My move to Lead Enterprise Architect was my experience coming full circle from Infrastructure back to Applications. Currently I am involved in (lots of learning) Identity Management,Mobility,SOA,ESB & UML training (we built the course & are teaching it in-house to IT). My cross-road now is whether to go down the management path or stay on the technical path. Specialties: SAN Architecture, data center management, replication, vendor relations, storage design, fabric design, high availability, high performance, high scalability
...

As a Java Full Stack Developer with over 5 years of hands-on experience, I architect and build the backbone of enterprise-grade applications. My passion lies in transforming complex business challenges into scalable, high-performance software solutions, from cloud-native microservices to intelligent data pipelines. I thrive on optimizing systems, automating processes, and delivering tangible results that drive business forward. At Norfolk Southern Railways, I engineered a microservices backend using Java 17 and Spring Boot that delivered RESTful APIs so efficient they slashed maintenance ticket turnaround by 620 hours annually. I also automated critical data workflows with Jakarta JMS and ActiveMQ, completely eliminating 87 manual interventions every month and boosting operational reliability. My expertise extends across the full stack and the entire software lifecycle: 🔹 Cloud & DevOps: I am proficient in architecting and deploying applications on AWS, GCP, and Azure, using containerization tools like Docker and Kubernetes to build resilient, scalable systems. I have streamlined CI/CD pipelines with Jenkins, GitHub Actions, and Terraform, significantly accelerating deployment cycles and reducing production rollbacks. 🔹 Data Engineering & Backend Systems: I have a deep understanding of data-intensive applications, having designed event-driven systems with Apache Kafka capable of processing billions of messages daily. ### ThoughtCraft: AI-Powered Blog Generator (GPT-4o | FastAPI | MongoDB)
...
Developed a full-stack web application to master generative AI frameworks. ThoughtCraft transforms long-form text into blog-ready output with summaries, SEO-friendly titles, tags, and structured content. Supports BART, Zephyr-7B (Hugging Face), LlamaIndex with RAG, and OpenAI’s GPT-4o for robust text processing. Built with FastAPI for high-performance APIs, Streamlit and Jinja2 for an interactive UI, MongoDB (via motor) for storage, and Pydantic for validation. Switched to GPT-4o due to CPU limitations and NVIDIA GPU issues (driver mismatches). Deployed on Streamlit Cloud for scalable access. Technologies: FastAPI, LangChain, LlamaIndex, OpenAI, Hugging Face Transformers, Streamlit, Jinja2, MongoDB, Pydantic, python-dotenv Show less

# Experienced Data Scientist – AI/Machine Learning Developer – Generative AI Platform Development
...
Join arenaflex, and you'll be part of a team that's changing the world one innovation at a time. We are currently seeking a talented and passionate Data Scientist to join our Cloud Technologies Application Platform team and help us build the future of developer experience through cutting-edge artificial intelligence solutions.
...
Are you ready to take your career to the next level with a company that values innovation and excellence? arenaflex is looking for an experienced Data Scientist to join our dynamic team as an AI/Machine Learning Developer. In this role, you will be instrumental in designing and executing our artificial intelligence strategy to elevate our developer experience platform to unprecedented heights.
...
This is a unique opportunity to work at the forefront of generative AI technology, collaborating with multidisciplinary teams to develop innovative solutions that will transform how developers create applications at arenaflex. You'll leverage your expertise in machine learning, deep learning, and large language models to solve complex business challenges and drive measurable impact across the organization.
...
As a Data Scientist at arenaflex, you will be responsible for driving our AI initiatives and contributing to the development of next-generation developer tools. Your key responsibilities will include:
...
Design, develop, and implement machine learning models and algorithms that enhance our developer experience platform, ensuring alignment with arenaflex's overall technology strategy.
...
Build, train, and fine-tune machine learning models, including foundation models and large language models, to address various business problems and improve developer productivity.
...
Choose appropriate machine learning algorithms, train models, and calibrate hyperparameters to achieve optimal predictive performance and model accuracy.
...
Apply deep expertise in transformer architecture, vector embeddings, and advanced ML techniques to develop solutions that solve complex business challenges.
...
Lead model deployment for experimentation, implementing A/B testing, monitoring model performance, and iterating on solutions based on empirical results.
...
Work closely with AI engineers, software developers, product managers, and other data scientists to deliver end-to-end AI-enabled solutions.
...
Experience with training models or utilizing foundation models and fine-tuning them to meet custom business requirements.
...
field. A Master's degree is strongly preferred and will be given special consideration.
...
Minimum of 5+ years of progressive experience in the software industry with a focused specialization in machine learning and artificial intelligence.
...
Strong understanding and extensive experience with cutting-edge machine learning algorithms, deep learning architectures, statistical modeling, and large language models.
...
- Advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in Data Science, Computer Science, or related field

- Experience applying machine learning techniques specifically in developer productivity or software development lifecycle contexts

- Knowledge of cloud platforms and infrastructure (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud)

- Familiarity with MLOps practices and deployment pipelines

- Published research or contributions to open-source machine learning projects

- Experience with prompt engineering and LLM optimization techniques
...
If you are passionate about artificial intelligence, machine learning, and want to work on projects that will shape the future of technology, arenaflex is the place for you. We are looking for individuals who bring enthusiasm, dedication, and fresh perspectives to their work – people who are ready to push boundaries and achieve extraordinary results.
